content.full_text:
Fern PolrrrcAr- Pnecrrcns C oMMrssroN
"128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacrarnento. CA 95gl.l-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 29,2009
Michael B. Montgomery
1933 Mill Road
South Pasadena, CA 91030
RE
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-158
Dear Mr. Montgomery:
This letter is in response to your request for advice dated June B, ZOO} regardine the
duties of the City of South Pasadena under the Political Reform Act (the .,Act',).f
Please note, pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B) (copy enclosed), unless you are the
authorized representative of the person whose duties you requeii advice about, the Fair political
Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. Moreover, your
question involves conduct that has already occurred, and the Commiision does not piovide
advice on past conduct. (see, section 831la@); Regulation 1g329(b)(gxA)&(c).)
In addition, your question primarily concems laws that are currently outside the
jurisdiction of Commission regulations. While the Act does require "o.*itt"., to report receipt
of contributions from state or local govemment agencies, and requires that if a state or local
government agency has enough campaign activity to qualify as a committee, the state or local
government agency itself will be required to file campaign statements,2 Commission regulations
do not currently prohibit the expenditure. Under many clrcumstances, such activities miy be
lle,sal under other provisions of the law. (see, penar Code, * 424;Gov. code, gg g314 and
54964.)
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 I 000 through g l0 I4. All statutory
references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Foi. politi.ul practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through I8997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory
references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
See Regulation l8'420; Wintler Advice Letter, No. I-08-021, copy enclosed. If you believe rhere has been
a violation of the Act and desire to file a complaint, visit the Commission;s website at the fbllowing link:
www.fppc.ca.gov.


Michael B. Montgomery
Page-2
Of some relevance, however, are proposed Commission Regulation 18901.1 and
proposed amendments to Regulation 18420.1 (copies enclosed). These regulations were
considered by the Commission at its June 2009 Commission Meeting and put over for further
consideration at its September 2009 meeting. We would welcome any comments you may have
on these proposals.
If you have other questions on this request for advice, please contact me. If you have
comments on the proposed regulations, you may contact Commission Counsel Brian Lau. We
both can be reached at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
Jwwjgl
Enclosures
